items.title: Principal Component Analysis
items.description: PCA finds orthogonal directions of maximum variance by eigendecomposing the covariance matrix (or SVD of centered data); eigenvalues rank how much variance each direction explains.

items.title: Graph spectral methods
items.description: Eigenvalues of graph Laplacians reveal community structure and connectivity; spectral clustering uses a few smallest non-zero eigenvectors as embedding coordinates.

items.title: Dynamical systems and Markov chains
items.description: Long-run behavior of linear iterations x_{k+1} = Ax_k and stationary distributions of Markov chains are governed by dominant eigenvalues and eigenvectors.

items.title: Numerical stability and conditioning
items.description: Eigenvalues of AᵀA relate to singular values; extreme spreads indicate ill-conditioning that affects least-squares solutions and optimization landscapes.
